name: Community Framework (CF)
description: Core dependency required by most DayZ mods. Provides shared libraries, configuration systems, and scripting APIs used across the modding ecosystem.
overview:
Community Framework (CF) is the foundational modding library for DayZ. Created and maintained by the DayZ modding community, it serves as a **universal dependency** that nearly every community-made mod requires.

### What CF Provides

- **Shared Scripting Libraries** — Common utility functions, data structures, and helper classes that mods use to avoid reinventing the wheel.
- **Configuration Framework** — A standardized system for reading/writing JSON configuration files, used by mods like Expansion, COT, and many others.
- **Module System** — A plugin-style architecture allowing mods to register themselves, declare dependencies, and hook into lifecycle events.
- **Permissions System** — Low-level permission APIs that admin tools like COT build upon.
- **Keybinding & Input Handling** — Shared input management so mods can register custom keybinds without conflicting with each other.
- **UI Framework** — Base UI classes and dialog systems used by mod GUIs.

### Why It Matters

Without CF, the vast majority of DayZ mods simply will not load. It must always be loaded **first** in your mod order (before all other community mods).

> **Note:** CF is a client-and-server mod — it must be installed on both the server and all connecting clients.
installation: Subscribe to Community Framework on the Steam Workshop (Workshop ID: 1559212036).
The mod will auto-download to your Steam Workshop content folder.
Copy or symlink the @CF folder to your DayZ server root directory.
Add @CF to your server's -mod= startup parameter. It must be listed BEFORE any mods that depend on it.
CF is also required on the client side — players must subscribe to it as well.
Restart your server. CF will generate its configuration files on first boot.

troubleshooting.solution: Ensure CF is loaded first in -mod= order, before any mods that depend on it.
Update CF to the latest version. Some mods require specific CF versions.
